default_rationale: PC in checkpoint/cursor contexts defaults to native program counter. All committed source evidence (IR compiler, checkpoint module, runtime, trace) uses 'pc' as a zero-based instruction index. No repository evidence proves an alternative scope (Parity Corrective, control plane, or otherwise).

decisions.rationale: All repository evidence — the Native IR compiler, checkpoint module, runtime, and trace modules — defines ``pc`` as a zero-based program counter indexing the instruction tuple. No committed evidence supports Parity Corrective or control-plane ownership of the ``pc`` field. The migration matrix row 4 (Native persistence checkpoint/cursor) is classified as residual/legacy under Native Platform ownership with M8 handoff. Row 91 (PC adjacent work) explicitly requires human portfolio gate approval — that gate is encoded as a blocker below, not accepted scope expansion.

source_files.quote: Each instruction carries an explicit program counter (``pc``)
source_files.interpretation: pc is a zero-based index into the instruction tuple

source_files.quote: The ``native`` key carries the program counter and a schema version
source_files.interpretation: cursor_pc / pc in checkpoint JSON refers to program counter

migration_matrix_rows.current_authority: program counter / Parity Corrective / control plane ambiguous

blockers.description: Human portfolio gate approval required for PC scope decision (migration matrix row 91, owner: Portfolio gate). Until approved, PC scope defaults to program counter only.
